repo: any repo with weak documentation and inconsistent structure
description: Tasks for validating repomind on a 'messy' repo: one where source files are distributed across multiple directories without clear domain keywords, docs are sparse, and the module structure is flatter than ideal. Werkzeug is chosen because it has a single src/werkzeug/ package with heterogeneous concerns (routing, serving, exceptions, datastructures, security, test) — hard to classify via directory keywords alone. Expected: classification should degrade gracefully, not crash.

expected_classification.notes: On a flat/mixed repo the classifier may produce 0 features; flat_rescue.py should provide fallback groupings. Key acceptance bar: no crash, at least 1 module detected.

tasks.description: Fix a bug in Werkzeug's URL routing and pattern matching
tasks.notes: Routing lives in src/werkzeug/routing/ — a subpackage. Should be detected as a module.

tasks.description: Add a new HTTP exception class to Werkzeug
tasks.notes: src/werkzeug/exceptions.py — flat file in root package

tasks.description: Update how Werkzeug handles multipart form data
Improve security in Werkzeug's HMAC-signed cookie utilities
Debug the Werkzeug development server's reloader

interpretation.pass_bar: No crash; at least 1 classified area; explain produces non-empty output for any target
interpretation.fail_signal: Zero features AND zero modules; or crash during init; or all context packs empty
interpretation.known_challenge: Flat single-package repos are the hardest case. The flat_rescue module should activate and provide keyword-based groupings as fallback.
